title: DSDS Document Block — Scoped Unions
description: Defines scoped document block unions, one per artifact type. Each piece of structured docs on an artifact is a block object with a `kind` tag. Each artifact type accepts its own special block kinds plus every general kind (see `generalDocumentBlock`). General kinds work on every artifact, including the free-form `sections` block. Special kinds are scoped, so you can't attach docs that don't fit — like an API spec on a foundation. Put internal artifact links in the shared `links` array on each entity, not in a block kind.

generalDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind available on every artifact type. General blocks cover concerns that apply to any artifact, including free-form prose. Every scoped union includes these kinds by pointing to this definition.

componentDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on component artifacts. Includes the component-specific kinds (imports, anatomy, API, variants, states, design specs) plus every general kind.

foundationDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on foundation artifacts. Includes the foundation-specific kinds (principles, scales, motion) plus every general kind.

patternDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on pattern artifacts. Includes the pattern-specific kind (interactions), shared structural kinds (anatomy, variants, states), and every general kind.

tokenDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on token, token-group, and theme artifacts. These accept the general kinds only (see `generalDocumentBlock`).

guideDocumentBlock.description: A document block kind accepted on guide artifacts. It includes the guide-only `steps` kind, the reused `imports` kind for setup and install steps, and every general kind. One of those is the free-form `sections` block, the backbone of a guide. Guides are made for reading, so their blocks favor prose and steps over the measurable specs (api, anatomy, design specs) that components use.
